initiatives.name: The Reclamation Project
initiatives.description: Youth-run grassroots organization building mutual aid initiatives, political education, and community healing programs for marginalized youth, with a focus on BIPOC, immigrant/refugee, and LGBTQ+ communities.

initiatives.name: Lavender Library
initiatives.description: Community-run LGBTQ+ library and archive providing free access to queer literature, history, and cultural resources through mutual aid and solidarity.

initiatives.name: Cellblock 2 Cityblock
initiatives.description: Re-entry support organization helping formerly incarcerated people transition back to the community through mutual aid and solidarity.

initiatives.name: Camp Resolution
initiatives.description: A self-governing homeless encampment in Sacramento that provides a safe, community-run space for unhoused individuals with support from local nonprofits and the city.

initiatives.name: Sacramento Homeless Union
initiatives.description: We are a 100% grassroots, community-funded, humanitarian group committed to providing equipment, clothing, and supplies to folks living on the streets.

initiatives.name: Sacramento Punks with Lunch
initiatives.description: A mutual aid collective providing direct support and harm reduction services in Sacramento. They coordinate food distribution, resource sharing, and harm reduction supplies while building networks of solidarity through street outreach and community organizing.

initiatives.name:
Anti Police-Terror Project (APTP)
              Sacramento
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization coordinating mutual aid and community response initiatives in Sacramento. They organize direct support through Mental Health First (MH First) crisis response, community education, and solidarity networks while building alternatives to policing through collective action and transformative justice.

initiatives.name: Sacramento Peace Action
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and community power through peace education and nonviolent action in Sacramento. They coordinate direct action, political education, and solidarity networks while building power through anti-war activism, social justice organizing, and creating spaces for movement building.

initiatives.name: SURJ Sacramento
initiatives.description: A chapter of Showing Up for Racial Justice fostering mutual aid and community solidarity through white anti-racist organizing in Sacramento. They coordinate political education, direct action support, and solidarity networks while building power through racial justice education and accountable organizing in partnership with BIPOC-led organizations like CHIRP (California Heritage: Indigenous Research Project) and NorCal Resist.

initiatives.name: Sacramento Food Not Bombs
initiatives.description: Serving a free vegan meal the 2nd and 4th Sunday of the month at 1:30pm on the corner of 10th & J. If you're interested in joining, message us!

initiatives.name: Sacramento Valley IWW
initiatives.description: We are a worker-led union dedicated to direct action, workplace democracy, and industrial unionism.

initiatives.name: Black Lives Matter Sacramento
initiatives.description: Racial justice organization that also hosts a community garden
